content_hints.description: The Sea Maidens Faire is the grandest parade of the fall season, and Jarlaxle seeks to have it as part of the Day of Wonders. The event takes place on the pier, where performers practice routines and caged creatures are offloaded, before the parade marches through Waterdeep’s streets.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see the harbor pier stretching ahead, slick with salt spray, where three gaudy ships ride at anchor beneath banners of crimson and gold. Calliope music drifts across the water, tinny and bright, and colored lanterns sway on lines strung between the masts.

npcs.name: Bregan D’aerthe
npcs.role: is using one of Jarlaxle’s legitimate business enterprises — the Sea Maidens Faire — as a front in Waterdeep.

event_triggers: On entry: The carnival is winding down for the evening. Colored lanterns sway on lines between the three ships, and the last acrobats are taking their bows. A figure in a wide-brimmed hat watches from the Eyecatcher's quarterdeck.

content_hints.description: The Sea Maidens Faire is a grand parade of the fall season, and Jarlaxle desires it to be a part of the Day of Wonders. During this event, performers practice their routines, and caged creatures are offloaded one by one.
content_hints.read_aloud: You step aboard the Eyecatcher and immediately notice it is no ordinary carnival ship. Caged creatures line the port rail—something dark and sleek chases its own tentacles in one cage, a reptilian thing clucks irritably in another, and a small winged lizard dozes on a velvet cushion. The whole vessel smells of exotic spice and fresh lacquer, and everything feels like a stage set—beautiful, deliberate, and designed to keep your eyes on the wrong thing.

npcs.name: Zardoz Zord
npcs.role: serves as grand marshal, leading the parade on the back of a rainbow-feathered diatryma summoned using Neverember’s hidden cache of dragons.

event_triggers: On conversation (DC 18 Insight): Zord's accent slips for half a syllable -- something clipped and precise beneath the bravado. His eyes, for just a moment, are the wrong color.
On investigation (DC 20): The Eyecatcher rides too low in the water for a ship this size. Something heavy is underneath.

content_hints.description: The Sea Maidens Faire is a seafaring carnival traveling up and down the Sword Coast in three ships: the ostentatious flagship, the Eyecatcher; the Heartbreaker, used for transporting performers and strange creatures; and the Hellraiser, which carries musicians and decorative floats. All three ships are crewed by drow who are magically disguised as slender humans and are loyal members of Bregan D’aerthe, all knowing that Zardoz Zord is Jarlaxle Baenre in disguise.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see the captain's quarters are lavishly appointed, with tapestries of desert scenes hanging beside curtains of shimmering black silk that seems to drink the lamplight. The air smells of wine, ink, and a cologne that carries notes of something foreign and deep—earth and spice from a place that has never seen the sun.

npcs.name: Jarlaxle Baenre
npcs.role: Jarlaxle’s seafaring carnival travels up and down the Sword Coast in three ships.

npcs.name: Velgos Ephezzrin
npcs.role: captains the Hellraiser in the guise of a human named Fergus Crabwater.

npcs.name: Tylan Ilueph
npcs.role: captains the Heartbreaker as a human named Klarr Besham.

hazards.name: Glyph of Warding on desk drawer

event_triggers: On search (DC 15 Investigation): The shipping manifests reveal cargo deliveries to four noble houses -- all members of the Lords' Alliance.
On search (DC 18 Investigation): The hidden floor panel is found, revealing a ladder descending to the submarine dock.
content_hints.investigation_success: The manifests reveal a pattern of deliveries to Lords' Alliance members -- Jarlaxle is mapping their weaknesses.

content_hints.description: The Sea Maidens Faire consists to gate the following morning shortly afterdawn. The griffon and its trainer normally reside in Peaktop Aerie atop Mount Waterdeep, but most civilians are not welcome there.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see hammocks swinging from the beams in tight rows, swaying with the ship's gentle motion. The crew quarters are cramped and dim, lit by a single shuttered lantern, and the air smells of oilcloth, salt, and something faintly chemical.

npcs.name: Bregan D’aerthe
npcs.role: is using one of Jarlaxle’s legitimate business enterprises — the Sea Maidens Faire — as a front in Waterdeep.

event_triggers: On combat: The drow fight in eerie silence, coordinating with hand signals. They aim to incapacitate, not kill -- Jarlaxle wants prisoners, not corpses.

content_hints.description: Where the tunnel branches, a stone bridge spans the sewage pouring out of an eastern artery. There is no walkway in this arterial passage that leads to areas B3 and B4.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see a circular shaft drop through the ship's keel, and halfway down the timber walls give way to riveted steel. Metal rungs are cold beneath your hands. At the bottom, a pressurized hatch opens with a hiss of escaping air, and you step into a chamber lit by lanterns sealed behind thick glass. The walls are iron, curved, and slick with condensation. The air tastes of machine oil and something else—something that vibrates faintly in your teeth. The floor hums. You are no longer aboard a ship. You are inside something else entirely.
event_triggers: On entry: The hatch seals behind you with a hiss of escaping air. A mechanical clicking begins somewhere in the walls -- the submarine is preparing to dive.

content_hints.description: The Day of Wonders is the grandest parade of the fall season, and Jarlaxle wants the Sea Maidens Faire to be a part of it. In the guise of Zardoz Zord, he pays a visit the day before the holiday to the House of Inspired Hands, Waterdeep’s temple of Gond, to coordinate with the acolytes in charge of organizing the parade.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see ps of paper, twisted pieces of metal, and bird droppings littering the space. Rocking chairs and a trellis table sit amidst the charred remains of a bookshelf, all shrouded in thick cobwebs. Arrow slits line the walls, and pigeons nest in the rafters above.

hazards.name: Alarm ward on Vault map case

event_triggers: On map read: The map reveals that Jarlaxle has already located the Vault of Dragons -- he just needs the dragonstaff of Ahghairon to open it.

content_hints.description: The Sea Maidens Faire is a grand parade of the fall season, and Jarlaxle desires it to be a part of the Day of Wonders. During this event, performers practice their routines, and caged creatures are offloaded one by one.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see the Sea Maidens Faire on the pier, a hive of activity where performers practice their routines and caged creatures are offloaded one by one. An hour before high sun on this crisp, windy autumn day, the parade makes its way to the House of Inspired Hands to meet up with the Gond worshipers and their contraptions.

npcs.name: Zardoz Zord
npcs.role: serves as grand marshal, leading the parade on the back of a rainbow-feathered diatryma summoned using Neverember’s hidden cache of dragons.

event_triggers: On negotiation: Fel'rekt offers a deal -- Jarlaxle will meet the party peacefully if they surrender their weapons. He keeps his word. Jarlaxle always keeps his word. The trick is what he does not say.

content_hints.description: The Sea Maidens Faire is a front in Waterdeep, used by Bregan D’aerthe. A successful DC 15 Charisma (Deception or Persuasion) check and the mention of Zardoz Zord’s name is enough to gain an audience with a ship captain.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see the Sea Maidens Faire, a vibrant spectacle of ships and activity. The air is filled with the sounds of the sea and the bustle of the fair.

npcs.name: Captain Zardoz Zord
npcs.role: The Sea Maidens Faire and all its ships are under the command of Captain Zardoz Zord of Luskan.

event_triggers: On entry: 'Ah, there you are. I was beginning to think you would never find the submarine. Most do not. Please, sit -- wine? No? Straight to business, then. I can respect that.' He does not draw a weapon. He does not need to.

On negotiation: Jarlaxle offers a deal: he wants the cache of dragons to buy Luskan a seat in the Lords' Alliance. He does not want war. He does not want blood. He wants legitimacy. 'Every lord in that Alliance got their seat through violence or inheritance. I merely propose a third option: commerce.'

On combat: Jarlaxle fights like a storm -- rapier in one hand, flying daggers from the bracer in the other, always moving, always grinning. If reduced below 50 HP he vanishes (Cape of Mountebank) and the submarine begins to flood. 3 rounds to escape.

On defeat: Jarlaxle surrenders with a bow. 'Well played. I concede this round. But I suspect we shall meet again -- I always enjoy a worthy adversary.' He means it. He does not suffer fools, but he respects skill.

content_hints.description: The Sea Maidens Faire is a front in Waterdeep used by Bregan D’aerthe. A successful DC 15 Charisma (Deception or Persuasion) check and mentioning Zardoz Zord's name can gain an audience with a ship captain.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see the bustling activity on the pier, a hive of activity where performers practice their routines and caged creatures are offloaded one by one.

npcs.name: Bregan D’aerthe
npcs.role: is using one of Jarlaxle’s legitimate business enterprises — the Sea Maidens Faire — as a front in Waterdeep.

hazards.name: Flooded escape tube (Constitution save or 1 level exhaustion)

event_triggers: On escape during flood: The submarine groans around you as seawater pours through breached seams. The escape tube is your only option -- swim or drown.
